capture.cameraPose: Desktop browser viewport after the first worn-headset report, showing the ED bay, spatial EHR/dialogue/input panels, status strip, and admin-side trace controls.
capture.captureCommand: Chrome DevTools MCP take_screenshot for the selected localhost page.

notes: The scene communicates an emergency department station at a blocking level, but it still lacks dense ED context such as leads, IV supplies, bedside carts, wall gases, telemetry detail, and time-pressure cues.

The patient, nurse, and spouse remain capsule/head placeholders with no realistic faces, clothing, hand meshes, posture detail, pain expression, or procedural animation; this matches the operator's report that hand visuals are primitive.

The Simulated EHR, Live Dialogue, and Input Evidence spatial panels are visible in the desktop capture; the Input Evidence panel no longer collides with the actor silhouettes or wall clock from this camera pose.

Trace buttons, the status strip, and the Full VR affordance are visible in desktop browser mode, but the screenshot does not prove deliberate hand-select trace advancement, hand-gesture locomotion, controller latency, or in-headset copy/export behavior.

Text panels are separated from the main actors, but the spatial composition is still tuned for evidence capture rather than a final natural ED layout; actor scale and panel distance must be challenged again in a worn-headset run.

This is desktop Chrome DevTools visual iteration evidence only; it is not physical Quest proof, not immersive WebXR proof, not in-headset readability proof, not hand-tracking quality proof, and not production runtime readiness.
